We recommended and the:

Municipal Treasurer include in the RPCPPE the CIP accounts to properly verify the existence of the ongoing construction projects and submit he RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year; and
Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the CIPLC in the prescribed format in accordance with MNGAS to document all the necessary details of ongoing construction projects, so that they can be used as source documents in conducting reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Treasurer.

Since the Municipal Treasurer was on leave and unable to attend the meeting, the Municipal Accountant stated that she would relay the audit observations to the Municipal Treasurer and ensure compliance with the audit recommendation.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Engineering and Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned offices, agreed to closely coordinate and develop a catch-up plan to fast track the implementation of the intended projects of the financial assistance and the request for reallocation of funds, including unimplemented financial assistance from prior years, if necessary.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure to specify the exact date of recording in the JEV to establish the date of liquidation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a certification stating that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for as an attachment to the next cash advance disbursement voucher of the same accountable officer.

The Municipal Accountant expressed concerns regarding the issuance of certifications stating that previous cash advances have been fully liquidated, citing the large volume of daily transactions and the lack of personnel dedicated to monitoring cash advances. She further stated that she would consult with and benchmark with other LGUs to identify best practices in addressing this matter.

As provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001, the issuance of a certification by the Municipal Accountant, stating that previous cash advances have been fully liquidated and properly recorded in the books, is a mandatory documentary requirement. This serves as one of the internal control measures to ensure that no new cash advances shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been fully settled or properly accounted for.

Otherwise, the unused funds be returned to the respective source agencies in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002.

Recommend that the concerned Municipal Officials of Tabuelan shall review all resources received under trust fund accounts and revisit all the Memorandum of Agreement at hand to come an appropriate decision towards the immediate implementation of priority projects given by National Government Agencies and the Local Government Units.

The excess funds from completed projects or projects no longer feasible, we recommend that these should be returned to the source agency in compliance with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the Rules and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of funds Transferred to Implementing Agencies.
observations.managementAction: Will Utilize the funds through implementation of the intended projects of all financial assistance received under Trust fund
observations.reason: Remained not all fund for specific projects were implemented. As of December 31, 2016, the account has a balance of P20,973,109.31.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of the P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise, all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, in payment of personnel incentives and similar allowances strict compliance with the provisions of applicable Executive Order as may be implemented by pertinent Budget Circular and other laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: An appeal for notice of disallowance was made by the LGU.  Of the Php 558,000 disallowance, Php 134,000 settlement was made for those employees who have retired already
observations.reason: Awaiting for the result of their appeal with the Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that concerned management personnel should immediately stop the payment of clothing allowance to job order personnel to avoid personal liability for illegal disbursements and require the refund of disbursed funds amounting to P595,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Of the total disallowance of Php 595,000, Php 74,502 remains unpaid as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Monthly settlement from the Job order personnel were collected thru payroll deduction.
